inputs.label: Principal Amount
inputs.businessContext: Initial investment or loan amount. Standard financial practice.

inputs.label: Annual Interest Rate (%)
inputs.businessContext: Nominal annual interest rate. Based on market rates or contract terms.

inputs.label: Compounding Frequency
inputs.businessContext: Number of compounding periods per year. Common values: annually (1), semi-annually (2), quarterly (4), monthly (12), daily (365), or continuous.

inputs.label: Time Period
inputs.businessContext: Duration of investment or loan in years. Standard financial planning horizon.

inputs.label: Additional Contribution per Period
inputs.businessContext: Regular contribution added each compounding period. Common in savings plans.

inputs.label: Contribution Timing
inputs.businessContext: Whether contributions are made at the beginning or end of each period. Affects future value calculation.

rules: if additionalContribution > 0 then compoundingFrequency must be numeric (not 'continuous')

thresholds.annualInterestRate > 20: Warning: High interest rate may indicate risky investment.
thresholds.timePeriod > 30: Warning: Long time horizon increases uncertainty.
thresholds.additionalContribution > principal * 0.5: Warning: Contribution amount is large relative to principal.

formulas.futureValue: if compoundingFrequency == 'continuous' then principal * exp(annualInterestRate/100 * timePeriod) + additionalContribution * (exp(annualInterestRate/100 * timePeriod) - 1) / (exp(annualInterestRate/100 * 1) - 1) else let n = compoundingFrequency, r = annualInterestRate/100, t = timePeriod, pmt = additionalContribution, timing = contributionTiming == 'beginning' ? 1 : 0 in principal * (1 + r/n)^(n*t) + pmt * ((1 + r/n)^(n*t) - 1) / (r/n) * (1 + r/n)^timing

hiddenLossDrivers: if annualInterestRate > 20 then 'High interest rate may erode value'
if timePeriod > 30 then 'Long horizon increases risk'
suggestedActions: Consider increasing compounding frequency to maximize growth.
Evaluate contribution amount relative to financial goals.
